title: Add AWS Access Key Secrets to GitHub Repository for CI/CD
description:
Add AWS access key secrets to the GitHub repository settings to support GitHub Actions CI/CD deployments. The secrets are needed so that AWS credentials are not exposed in the `deploy.yml` workflow file.

## Required Secrets
- `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID_DEV_QA` — access key for dev/QA Lambda deployments
- `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY_DEV_QA` — secret key for dev/QA
- `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID_PROD` — access key for production Lambda deployments
- `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY_PROD` — secret key for production

The dev/QA keys are created for a user with full access to Lambda and other AWS services. The same credentials are used for dev/QA until a separate AWS account is created for production.
acceptance_criteria: Four AWS secrets are created in the GitHub repository settings
GitHub Actions deploy.yml references secrets instead of hardcoded credentials
Dev/QA deployments authenticate using the DEV_QA secret pair
Production deployments authenticate using the PROD secret pair
No AWS credentials are exposed in the repository code or workflow files
implementation_notes: Secrets configured per GitHub docs: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/security-guides/using-secrets-in-github-actions. Raj requested this setup for secure CI/CD pipeline deployment to Lambda. Temporary approach shares dev/QA credentials until separate prod AWS account is established.
